title: Dynamic network storage for cloud console server
abstract: Aspects of the present disclosure relate to systems and methods for virtualizing mass storage using a network storage device operatively coupled to a host system. In various implementations, the network storage device and host system may collectively emulate a mass storage device for another terminal computing system. This emulation can be performed while utilizing the minimum possible memory of the host system, as the network storage device contains the operating system data for the host system in addition to game and user data required for the implementation of a computer application.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system, comprising: a processor, and a memory coupled to the processor; wherein the processor is configured to perform a method, the method comprising: receiving, from a remote client computing device, a user request to stream a computer application; requesting data necessary to load and/or execute the computer application from a network storage device; receiving data necessary to run the computer application from the network storage device; running the computer application using the data; and streaming an output generated by running the computer application using the data to the remote client computing device. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the remote client computing device, system, and network storage device are connected to each other via one or more networks. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the network storage device is a storage server. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the network storage device contains encrypted application data that cannot be decrypted by the network storage device. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the system is capable of decrypting encrypted application data. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the network storage device contains user data. 7. The system of claim 6, further comprising analyzing the user data to determine if the user has permission to run the requested application. 8. The system of claim 6, wherein the network storage device is capable of retrieving additional user data from a save data server. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the network storage device is connected to the save data server via a network connection. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the network storage device contains operating system (OS) data for the system. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein the data necessary to run the computer application includes additional OS data for the system. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein the network storage device is automatically updated such that the data delivered to the system by the network storage device in response to a request is the most recent version of the requested data. 13. The system of claim 1, wherein the request to run a computer application is received by the system after being passed through a management server. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the management server is connected to the system and the network storage device over a network. 15. The system of claim 13, wherein the network storage device contains encrypted application data that cannot be decrypted by the management server. 16. The system of claim 13, wherein the management server prepares the system to deliver the output generated by running the computer application using the data to the remote client computing device. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the management server causes one or more components of the system to be powered in response to the request to run a computer application. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the system is powered by the management server using a hardware control interface. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the hardware control interface comprises toggling a power switch signal using general purpose input output (GPIO). 20. The system of claim 18, wherein the hardware control interface comprises sending a wake up signal over local area network (LAN). 21. The system of claim 18, wherein the hardware control interface comprises a universal asynchronous receiver/transmitter (UART) command port. 22. The system of claim 13, wherein the management server allocates resources of the network storage device for use by the system in response to the request to run a computer application. 23. The system of claim 13, wherein the management server initiates software updates of the system if a software update is necessary. 24. The system of claim 1, wherein the system is assigned to the remote client computing device by a cloud service server in response to a streaming request from the remote client computing device. 25. The system of claim 24, wherein the streaming request from the remote client computing device is delivered to the cloud service server through a streaming client utilized by the user. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein the cloud service server provides instructions to the streaming client to stream from the system. 27. On a server configured to operate on a network, a method, comprising: receiving, from a remote client computing device, a user request to stream a computer application; requesting data necessary to load and/or run the computer application from a network storage device; receiving data necessary to run the computer application from a network storage device; running the computer application using the data; and streaming an output generated by running the computer application using the data to the remote client computing device. 28. A non-transitory computer readable medium having processor-executable instructions embodied therein, wherein execution of the instructions by a processor cause the processor to implement a method for sharing assets, the method comprising: receiving, from a remote client computing device, a user request to stream a computer application; requesting data necessary to load and/or run the computer application from a network storage device; receiving data necessary to run the computer application from a network storage device; running the computer application; and streaming an output generated by running the computer application using the data to the remote client computing device.
